task_completion.description: The agent initiates the action to crop the image to the current selection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A menu is open (e.g., 'Image') with an option like 'Crop to Selection' highlighted or clicked, or a crop tool is actively being applied to the selected region.

task_completion.description: The image canvas is successfully cropped to the rectangular selection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image displayed in the editor is visibly smaller, containing only the contents of the previously selected rectangular region, with the rest of the original image removed.

task_completion.description: A dialog to save or export the cropped image is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As', 'Export', or 'Export As' dialog window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The correct destination directory (/home/ga/Documents) is selected in the save dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save/export dialog shows 'Documents' selected in the sidebar, or the file path explicitly shows '/home/ga/Documents/' or '~/Documents/'.
The name field in the save/export dialog contains exactly 'cropped.png'.

task_completion.description: The agent confirms the save/export action.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor is clicking the 'Save' or 'Export' button in the dialog, or a subsequent screenshot shows the dialog closed after the correct path and filename were set.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of an image editor to crop the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The cropping action occurs within an image editing application's UI, rather than a terminal window using command-line image processing tools (like ImageMagick).

integrity.description: The agent used the application's save/export dialog to save the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is saved via the application's native dialog box, not by using terminal commands to move, rename, or copy files into the Documents directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a genuine image editing application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a real image editor (e.g., GIMP, Pinta, or similar) with standard image manipulation toolbars and menus, not a mock interface or text editor.
